1.notes: Straight opening par 4. Classic Donald Ross crowned green requires precise approach. Tree-lined fairway typical of Sedgefield.

2.notes: Par 5 dogleg left. Reachable in two for long hitters. Fairway bunker guards landing zone. Ross green complex.

3.notes: Uphill par 3 with bunkers guarding the front. Ross green slopes back to front.

4.notes: Par 4 dogleg right. Fairway bunker at the corner rewards an aggressive line. Two-tiered Ross green.

5.notes: Downhill par 5. Reachable for long hitters but narrow landing areas. Deep green with severe slopes.

6.notes: Gentle dogleg left par 4. Accuracy off the tee sets up second shot to crowned green.

7.notes: Short par 3 with bunkers on both sides. Small target requires precision. Classic Ross.

8.notes: Long par 4 demanding accuracy. Fairway bunker left. Kidney-shaped green with multiple pin positions.

9.notes: Uphill dogleg right finishing the front. Tiered green frames iconic Tudor clubhouse.

10.notes: Downhill start to back nine. Driver can be challenged by fairway bunker right.

11.notes: Mid-length par 3. Bunkers protect the green. Club selection critical.

12.notes: Long par 5 dogleg left. Multiple bunkers challenge both layups and going for it. Large kidney green.

13.notes: Dogleg right par 4. Fairway bunker guards corner. Precise approach needed to small Ross green.

14.notes: Long uphill par 4. Two-tiered green makes putting treacherous from wrong level.

15.notes: Straightforward par 4 offering birdie chance before tough finish. Classic Ross green.

16.notes: Par 3 over water. One of Sedgefield's most challenging holes. No bailout short.

17.notes: Par 5 birdie opportunity before 18. Reachable in two but fairway bunkers demand precision.

18.notes: Classic finishing hole uphill to clubhouse. Fairway bunkers pinch landing area. Brandt Snedeker shot 59 here (2018).

1.notes: Opening par 4 at Detroit Golf Club's North Course. Tree-lined fairway with Donald Ross green complex.

2.notes: Dogleg right par 4. Bunker guards corner. Two-tiered Ross green demands precise approach.

3.notes: Par 3 with bunkers short. Classic Ross crowned green.

4.notes: Long par 5 with slight left bend. Reachable for long hitters. Fairway bunkers challenge both shots.

5.notes: Dogleg left par 4. Fairway bunker punishes aggressive line. Ross green typical of DGC.

6.notes: Long par 4 requiring two solid shots. Two-tiered green challenges putting.

7.notes: Longer par 3 with bunkers left and right. Near the historic bent tree marking original Indian Trail.

8.notes: Par 5 dogleg right. Multiple bunkers challenge aggressive play. Signature hole at DGC.

9.notes: Tough finishing hole for the front. Fairway bunkers pinch landing area. Large Ross green.

10.notes: Par 4 to start back nine. Slight right bend with tree-lined fairway. Classic Ross routing.

11.notes: Long par 4 requiring power and accuracy. Fairway bunker left forces decision off tee.

12.notes: Short par 3 near site of original clubhouse. Bunkers guard crowned green.

13.notes: Par 5 dogleg left. Multiple fairway bunkers. Near combined tee with South Course 14th.

14.notes: Mid-length par 4. Fairway bunker guards left side. Ross green demands precision.

15.notes: Dogleg right par 4. Corner bunker punishes aggressive line. Two-tiered Ross green.

16.notes: Par 3 with bunkers guarding. Club selection key on this medium-length one-shotter.

17.notes: Par 5 birdie chance before challenging 18. Reachable but requires accurate layup or bold second.

18.notes: Demanding finishing par 4. Fairway bunkers pinch landing area. Tiered green toward historic Albert Kahn clubhouse.

1.notes: Opening par 4 at TPC Craig Ranch. Tom Weiskopf design. Generous fairway to start.

2.notes: Long par 4 with slight left bend. Water right of approach. Challenging two-tiered green.

3.notes: Dogleg right par 4. Multiple fairway bunkers. Kidney-shaped Weiskopf green.

4.notes: Par 3 over water. No bailout short. One of TPC Craig Ranch's signature holes.

5.notes: Monster par 5 at 635 yards. Dogleg left with water down left side. Reachable only for longest hitters.

6.notes: Shorter par 4 birdie opportunity. Straightforward but green slopes demand precision.

7.notes: Long par 3 with bunkers front and back. No bailout. Demands precise club selection.

8.notes: Long par 4 dogleg right. Water on approach. Two-tiered green makes putting tricky.

9.notes: Par 5 finishing front nine. Dogleg left with water on approach. Birdie opportunity for aggressive play.

10.notes: Long par 4 to start back nine. Fairway bunkers pinch landing area. Demanding approach.

11.notes: Long par 4 with slight right bend. Water guards right side of approach. Challenging second shot.

12.notes: Long par 4 dogleg left. Water left on approach. Weiskopf demands precision here.

13.notes: Par 4 playing over 500 yards. Fairway bunkers both sides pinch landing. Kidney green offers options.

14.notes: Short par 4 birdie opportunity. Allows aggressive players to attack before tough finish.

15.notes: Par 3 over water. No bailout short. Must commit to the carry.

16.notes: Long par 4 dogleg right. Water right guards approach. Two-tiered green challenges putts.

17.notes: Signature short par 3. Island green surrounded by water. All or nothing. Premium on accuracy.

18.notes: Finishing par 5 dogleg left. Reachable for eagles but water guards left. Risk/reward to close CJ Cup Byron Nelson.
